title: 10 possible side effects of Scutellaria baicalensis (Baical Skullcap) if used improperly
intro: Scutellaria baicalensis, also known as Chinese skullcap, is a traditional herbal remedy that can have potential negative health consequences if used improperly. One possible side effect is liver toxicity, which can cause symptoms such as jaundice, fatigue, and abdominal pain. Improper use may also lead to allergic reactions, including rashes, itching, and difficulty breathing. Additionally, this herb can interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and sedatives, potentially leading to adverse effects. Pregnant or breastfeeding women should avoid using Scutellaria baicalensis, as it may cause harm to the fetus or infant. Lastly, overdosing on Scutellaria baicalensis can result in digestive issues like nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as dizziness and muscle spasms.
